title: component_previews data model and migration
description: Add the `component_previews` table (`id, component_name, variant_id, commit_sha, path_estatico, publicado_em`) to the Drizzle schema, tracking every published preview artifact version.
acceptanceCriteria: `component_previews` table exists with `id, component_name (text), variant_id (text), commit_sha (text), path_estatico (text), publicado_em (timestamp)`
A query helper exists to fetch the *latest* `component_previews` row for a given `(component_name, variant_id)` pair, ordered by `publicado_em` descending

No uniqueness constraint blocks multiple historical entries for the same `(component_name, variant_id)` — history is intentionally append-only (each publish is a new row), enabling 'latest wins' resolution
Migration applies cleanly on top of prior migrations

steps.details: Define `componentPreviews` with `id`, `componentName: text('component_name').notNull()`, `variantId: text('variant_id').notNull()`, `commitSha: text('commit_sha').notNull()`, `pathEstatico: text('path_estatico').notNull()`, `publicadoEm: integer('publicado_em', { mode: 'timestamp' }).notNull()`.

steps.description: Add an index for latest-lookup performance
steps.details: Add a Drizzle index on `(component_name, variant_id, publicado_em)` to make the 'latest per component+variant' query efficient.
In `apps/server/src/db/componentPreviews.ts`, add `getLatestPreview(componentName: string, variantId: string)` returning the most recent row for that pair, or `null` if none exists.
Run `drizzle-kit generate`, then insert two rows for the same `(component_name, variant_id)` with different `publicado_em`, and confirm `getLatestPreview` returns the more recent one.

technicalNotes: Keeping this table append-only (rather than upserting) preserves a full history of preview publishes per component/variant, which is useful for debugging 'why did my preview change' even though the MVP UI only surfaces the latest.
